destinations.evidence: Malformed HTTP input, invalid payloads, zero executor, and executor mismatches become invalid async commitment errors before SPC verification can use a fallback executor.
When the block verifier is configured, invalid commitment/SPC verification errors reject block execution instead of allowing fallback verification.

equivalence_argument: Malformed HTTP executor input is rejected by canonical block verification, and the dependency executor converts verifier failure into BlockExecutionError; no path verifies malformed HTTP SPC input with a fallback executor.
notes: Mapped in port with dependency execution plumbing.
rationale: The new code converts a missing extracted executor into `BlockExecutionError::msg` with text saying the block should be rejected. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: If an HTTP SPC call is present but its input data does not contain an extractable executor address, block execution fails with a block rejection error instead of verifying with a fallback executor.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build a block with an HTTP SPC call whose input omits or corrupts the executor address and assert execution returns a block execution error before creating an SPC verification request.
summary: Malformed HTTP executor inputs are rejected by block verification.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Build a block with an HTTP SPC call whose input omits or corrupts the executor address and assert execution returns a block execution error before creating an SPC verification request.
title: Malformed HTTP SPC executor rejects block
